title: APIs
description: What APIs are and how the web uses them to share data.

blocks.content:
An **Application Programming Interface (API)** is a defined way for two pieces of software to communicate. One program makes a **request**, and the other responds with **data** or an action.

On the web, APIs are used everywhere. When you check the weather, load a map, or log in with Google, your browser is talking to an API behind the scenes.

A **web API** (also called a REST API) communicates over the internet using URLs. You request a specific URL called an **endpoint**, and the server responds with data — almost always in JSON format.

For example, the public DummyJSON API exposes this endpoint:

```
https://dummyjson.com/users/1
```

Requesting that URL returns a JSON object containing information about user number 1.
Every API request uses an **HTTP method** that describes what kind of action is being performed. The most common methods are:

blocks.title: In this module we focus on GET
blocks.content: The fetch() function defaults to a GET request, which is used to read data. You will learn how to send other methods later in your studies.

An **endpoint** is a specific URL that the API exposes. Different endpoints return different data. Many APIs also accept **query parameters** — extra values appended to the URL after a `?` — to filter or customise the response.

https://dummyjson.com/products          // all products
https://dummyjson.com/products/5        // product with id 5
https://dummyjson.com/products?limit=3  // only 3 products
blocks.caption: Three endpoints for the same API, each returning different data

blocks.title: APIs Quiz
blocks.description: Test your understanding of APIs and HTTP methods.

questions.question: What does API stand for?
questions.explanation: API stands for Application Programming Interface — a way for software to communicate.

questions.question: Which HTTP method should you use to read data from an API?
questions.explanation: GET is used to retrieve/read data. It is the default method used by fetch().

questions.question: In what format do most web APIs return their data?
questions.explanation: The vast majority of modern web APIs return data in JSON format.

questions.question: What is an API endpoint?
options: The error message returned when a request fails
A specific URL that the API exposes to receive requests
The JavaScript function used to send requests
The server that hosts the API
questions.explanation: An endpoint is a specific URL path on the API server that accepts requests and returns data.
